content: Lymphomas are malignancies of lymphocytes classified as Hodgkin lymphoma (HL) or non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL). Hodgkin lymphoma: characterized by Reed-Sternberg cells (CD30+, CD15+, PAX5 weak+); subtypes include classical HL (nodular sclerosis, mixed cellularity, lymphocyte-rich, lymphocyte-depleted) and nodular lymphocyte-predominant HL (NLPHL, CD20+, 'popcorn cells'). Non-Hodgkin lymphoma: diverse group (>60 subtypes). B-cell NHL (85%): diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL, most common, aggressive), follicular lymphoma (indolent, t(14;18) BCL2), mantle cell lymphoma (t(11;14) cyclin D1, aggressive), marginal zone lymphoma (indolent), Burkitt lymphoma (t(8;14) MYC, very aggressive), CLL/SLL. T-cell NHL (15%): peripheral T-cell lymphoma NOS, anaplastic large cell lymphoma (ALCL, CD30+, ALK+ or ALK-), angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (mycosis fungoides, Sézary syndrome).
